rationale: In the conceptual framing, the cited study is used as a concrete prior example of stigma-related threat/disrespect cues in intergroup marketplace settings (low-SES consumers in high-end environments). The citing paper explicitly positions its own contribution as expanding this stream to a different cue: a firm's discounting of stigmatized-identity-linked products. This role is reiterated in the general discussion, where the paper contrasts prior interaction-based cues (including Jacob et al. 2022) with its newly identified pricing-based cue.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Jacob et al. (2022) as prior evidence that stigmatized consumers respond to marketplace devaluation cues, then extends that line by examining a new trigger: discounts on products symbolically linked to stigmatized identities. It broadens the phenomenon from SES intergroup shopping contexts to multiple stigmatized identities and promotion settings.
